# GMEPay+ — WBS Status & Completion Audit

> **Refreshed 2026-06-16** with a capability-weighted completion audit (PRD + business
> case vs WBS vs *current* code, 12 capability domains, every % adversarially re-graded).
> The ticket-level burndown in **Section 2** is the earlier script-generated snapshot; it
> is now conservative for the money-path services because the P0/P1 work (payment
> orchestration, gateway security filters, webhook dispatcher, partner config, admin-ui
> validation) landed *after* that snapshot was produced.

---

## Section 1 — Capability completion (2026-06-16)

### Two lenses, both true

| Lens | Figure |
|---|---|
| **Capability-weighted complete** (avg across 12 domains) | **~49%** |
| **MVP / go-live-critical complete** (avg across the 9 money-path domains) | **~56%** |
| **Ticket-count complete** (Done+Partial of 3,444 WBS tickets — Section 2) | **~10%** |

The spread is expected and not a defect. The **ticket count** is dominated by ~thousands of
not-started infrastructure, observability, security-program, QA and (calendar-bound)
regulatory tickets that are correctly back-loaded. **Capability-weighting** measures whether
each *business capability* actually functions. Net: **roughly half a working product —
concentrated in the money-path — sitting on ~10% of the total ticketed scope.**

### Capability completion by domain (verifier-adjusted)

| Domain | MVP-critical | Capability % | MVP-slice % | State |
|---|:--:|--:|--:|---|
| Transaction lifecycle | ✅ | **66** | 68 | Guarded FSM, status-PATCH, 60s sweeper, outbox — real & tested |
| Partner & config management | ✅ | **66** | 66 | 8-step wizard + 4-eyes + hash-chained audit; seams stubbed, not run e2e w/ data |
| UIs (admin / portal / wizard) | ✅ | **60** | 53 | Most complete by surface; data synthetic (stub BFF), refund/quote-health UIs missing |
| Prefunding & balances | ✅ | **60** | 63 | Atomic SELECT-FOR-UPDATE proven on PG IT; low-balance alert never *delivered* |
| FX & rate | ✅ | **56** | 64 | RECEIVE-mode USD-pool engine real; live rate feed not wired into quotes |
| API gateway & partner security | ✅ | **55** | 57 | All 6 filters real (HMAC live-verified); trust roots stubbed, no throttling/Redis |
| Payment & QR money-path | ✅ | **54** | 50 | MPM live-verified vs simulator; CPM is a stub; sim-only scheme; H2/in-memory |
| Settlement, recon & revenue | ✅ | **44** | 41 | Calculators + recon API real; settlement booking + outbound ZP files + SFTP missing |
| Notification & webhooks | ✅ | **44** | 46 | Signing/retry/DLQ/dispatcher built but no `payment.approved` is ever emitted (dark) |
| Auth/identity & platform security | ⬜ | **35** | 44 | Good primitives (PBKDF2/OIDC/hash-chain); issuance↔verify unwired; RBAC fixture-only |
| Compliance & regulatory reporting | ⬜ | **25** | 55 | BOK/Hometax/KoFIU coded but gated off; mostly post-go-live anyway |
| Infra, observability & QA | ⬜ | **21** | 29 | Compose + Testcontainers exist; no k8s/IaC/Vault/OTel/alerting/E2E-gate/perf/UAT |

**Overall: ~49% capability-weighted · ~56% MVP-critical.**

### Go-live (2026-10-10) readiness: **NOT READY**

The MVP money-path is demonstrable *in pieces against simulators on H2/in-memory* — a real
milestone, but not production-grade end-to-end. Critical-path blockers, in order:

1. **KFTC / 한결원 certification + real scheme protocol & SFTP** — external, calendar-bound,
   not started; gates *all* live domestic traffic. Highest schedule risk.
2. **Settlement batch lifecycle** — book/persist per-partner batches, generate + transmit the
   outbound ZeroPay files (ZP0011/0021/0061/0063/0065/0066) over SFTP, enforce
   "registration failure blocks settlement," turn on reconciliation. Largest unbuilt MVP capability.
3. **Stand up the real stack** (Postgres + Redis + Kafka + Keycloak) as the *default* runtime
   and prove producer → Kafka → consumer → **webhook** green (emit `payment.approved`).
4. **Real auth/RBAC** — retire `password=demo` and `StubPartnerCredentialService`; wire the
   hashed/revocable issued credentials; make the BFF a resource server; fix the OIDC client-id.
5. **Admin refund flow** (the only Phase-1 refund channel) + faithful **CPM** implementation.
6. **Security hardening** — gateway rate-limiting/throttling (absent), Redis-backed replay,
   fail-closed allowlist, secrets in a real vault.
7. **QA / observability / operability** — automated E2E money-path CI gate, OTel/Prometheus/
   Grafana + ELK + alerting, load test vs NFR, UAT sign-off, cutover/rollback runbook.

**Deferred / calendar-bound** (not Oct-10 blockers if launch stays domestic GME-Remit on
ZeroPay): BOK FX1014/1015 reporting (domestic same-ccy is exempt), Hometax tax-invoice,
KoFIU CTR/STR, Travel-Rule, PIPA/AML enforcement, Partner-B authoritative quote, and
operator-side scheme-profile config.

*Source: `prd-wbs-gap-completion-audit` workflow, 2026-06-16 — 12 domains assessed against
PRD.txt + BusinessScenario.txt + WBS + current code, each completion % adversarially verified.*

## Section 2 — Ticket-level burndown (script-generated snapshot)

Generated by walking every per-service ticket bundle in `Documentation/services_backlog/` and checking the corresponding code paths on disk.

### Headline numbers

- **Tickets audited:** 3,091 (of 3,444 in the backlog — see note below)
- **DONE (acceptance checks met):** **50** (2%)
- **PARTIAL (deliverable exists, some check unmet):** **256** (8%)
- **NOT STARTED:** **2,785** (90%)
- **Done + Partial combined:** **306** (10%)

*Note: The smart-router bundle was not audited (agent returned 0 tickets — the `5.4` work-package it was assigned to is actually QR-Parser work owned by `qr-service`, where its deliverables ARE counted). Some Addendum-001 tickets may also be missing — total audited 3,091 vs backlog 3,444.*

> **Reconcile with Section 1:** the `0% Done` rows for the money-path services (payment-executor,
> rate-fx, prefunding, transaction-mgmt, merchant-qr-data) understate reality — those tickets
> were scored before the P0/P1 work and against strict acceptance checks (Testcontainers
> Postgres, real Kafka). The capability audit credits the working H2/simulator implementations
> that those services now have.

### Common reasons tickets are PARTIAL rather than DONE

- **H2 in-memory instead of Testcontainers PostgreSQL** — the deliverable exists and works against H2 in PostgreSQL compatibility mode, but the ticket's acceptance check specified Testcontainers (we cannot run Docker in the build env).
- **In-memory store instead of Redis** — config cache / idempotency keys / rate-quote TTL implemented in-memory; Redis dep was declared but not wired.
- **Stub clients instead of real REST** — agents shipped `Stub*Client` @Component beans + `Rest*Client` @Primary impls; the REST impls compile but inter-service traffic hasn't been validated end-to-end against a running stack.
- **No real Kafka producer** — Outbox + `EventPublisher` interface + `LogEventPublisher` are wired; the Kafka producer impl is not.
- **OpenAPI declared but no OpenAPI-generated client used cross-service** — services hand-write DTOs that match each other; not generated from the spec.
- **Logic done, regulatory format not finalised** — e.g. FX1014/1015 mapping exists; the exact CSV/XML format is pending open item OI-03 from BOK.
- **Auth is Phase-1 stub** — login accepts any non-empty username with password=`demo`. Real OAuth2/JWT + RBAC via `auth-identity` is deferred to Phase 4 hardening.

### What this means

- **~10% (D+P) is genuine scaffolding progress** spread across the money-path services + the two UIs + the planning artifacts.
- **~90% NOT STARTED** is dominated by:
  - The compliance/regulatory tickets (KFTC certification, BOK reporting, tax-invoice — all calendar-bound by external parties).
  - The infrastructure/observability tickets (K8s, ArgoCD, Helm, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, Jaeger) — `platform-infra`'s 352 tickets are 95% not started.
  - The security tickets (Vault, mTLS, RBAC, pen test, audit pipeline) — `security-platform`'s 199 tickets are 94% not started.
  - The cross-cutting QA tickets (load, perf, contract, E2E, UAT) — `qa-platform`'s 365 tickets are 96% not started.
  - Many deep per-service feature tickets (e.g. `scheme-adapter-zeropay` has 234 tickets covering KFTC certification flow; only ~6% scaffolded).

### How to read the Backlog xlsx now

Two new columns on the `Backlog` sheet:
- **Status**: `DONE` (green), `PARTIAL` (amber), `NOT STARTED` (grey), `(no audit)` for the unmatched ~353 tickets.
- **Evidence**: one-line note — file path, endpoint name, or short reason.

The `By Microservice` sheet has been extended with 6 audit columns (Audited, Done, Partial, Not Started, % Done, % Done+Partial).
